name: resource-allocation
description: Analyze team capacity, optimize task assignments based on skills and workload, and detect over/under-allocation
objectives: Assess team capacity across active projects
Match task requirements to team member skills
Detect over-allocated and under-utilized team members
Recommend task reassignments to balance workload
Project capacity availability for upcoming work
prerequisites: Team member profiles with skills and roles exist
Tasks with assignments and effort estimates

procedure.description: Calculate available capacity per team member
commands: QUERY team members with roles and working hours
CALCULATE total available hours per person per week
SUBTRACT time for meetings, overhead, and leave
QUERY current task assignments with effort estimates
COMPUTE allocated hours vs available hours per person
procedure.expectedOutput: Capacity matrix: available vs allocated per team member

commands: CALCULATE workload percentage per person (allocated/available)
FLAG over-allocated members (>100% capacity)
FLAG under-utilized members (<50% capacity)
IDENTIFY workload imbalances across the team
CHECK for skill concentration risk (single points of failure)
MAP required skills per task to team member capabilities
IDENTIFY tasks assigned to people lacking required skills
FIND better skill matches for misaligned tasks
DETECT single points of failure (only 1 person with key skill)
SUGGEST cross-training opportunities
procedure.expectedOutput: Skill alignment report with reassignment suggestions

commands: GENERATE reassignment recommendations to balance workload
RESPECT skill requirements and dependencies
MINIMIZE disruption (prefer small moves over large reshuffles)
CALCULATE improvement metrics (before vs after balancing)
IDENTIFY tasks that could be deferred to reduce overload
procedure.expectedOutput: Rebalancing plan with specific reassignment proposals

procedure.description: Project future capacity for upcoming work
commands: PROJECT task completion dates based on current velocity
CALCULATE when team members will have freed capacity
IDENTIFY capacity windows for new project intake
FLAG upcoming capacity crunches or idle periods
RECOMMEND hiring or contracting needs

criteria: All team members accounted for in capacity calculation
Forecast aligns with known project timelines

testScenarios.test: Team member with tasks totaling 60 hours in a 40-hour week
Check capacity for a new 3-week project
testScenarios.expected: Available team members identified with capacity windows

successIndicators: Tasks completed on time due to proper resourcing

commonErrors.solution: Use default working hours, flag members needing updates
Apply average effort by task type, flag for estimation

escalationCriteria: Team-wide over-allocation with no rebalancing options
Critical skills unavailable for priority tasks

successMetrics.name: Team Utilization
successMetrics.measurement: Average allocated hours vs available hours

successMetrics.name: Over-Allocation Reduction
successMetrics.measurement: Team members exceeding capacity after optimization

successMetrics.name: Skill Match Rate
successMetrics.measurement: Tasks assigned to appropriately skilled members

agentInterfaces.message: Fetch team member profiles, skills, and availability
Fetch task assignments and effort estimates across projects
